text.title: Integration Testing Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every pytest-based integration suite MUST enforce. Hard rules — operator input cannot override them.

rule.statement: The default isolation strategy MUST be transaction rollback: open a connection, begin a transaction, yield a Session, then rollback unconditionally in teardown. Tests never commit.
rule.rationale: Rollback is one low-level operation; DELETE/TRUNCATE require per-table scans. The rollback path keeps function-scope teardown under 5 ms.

rule.statement: Testcontainers Docker containers MUST be session-scoped; database Session fixtures MUST be function-scoped.
rule.rationale: Container start costs 2-5 s. Session scope amortises it across the entire run while function scope keeps per-test isolation cheap.

rule.statement: Every test that uses `app.dependency_overrides[...]` MUST clear overrides in teardown (`app.dependency_overrides.clear()` or pop the specific key).
rule.rationale: Overrides are stored on the app object and leak between tests. Stale overrides produce mystery flakes where test order changes outcomes.

rule.statement: External HTTP calls MUST be intercepted: respx for transport-level mocking (Python async/sync), WireMock container when HTTP semantics (redirects, chunked transfer, retries) matter.
rule.rationale: Real third-party calls inject network flakiness and side-effects. respx is zero-overhead; WireMock catches connection-pool bugs that mock transports miss.

rule.statement: Tests running under parallel_target >= 2 MUST use UUID-based unique values for unique-constrained columns (email, username, idempotency_key). Factory Sequence is allowed only for serial runs.
rule.rationale: Sequence counters collide across xdist workers. UUIDs decouple workers without requiring cross-process coordination.

rule.statement: Tests MUST NOT connect to production databases or live external APIs. All secrets-redirect env vars are redirected to test containers or mock URLs in the conftest.
rule.rationale: CI runs concurrently with production traffic; mutating prod from a test is a sev-1 outage waiting to happen.

rule.statement: An integration test MUST exercise the real connection layer (DB driver, HTTP client). Mocking the SQLAlchemy session or the httpx client demotes the test to a unit test.
rule.rationale: The point of integration testing is the connection: ORM mapping, constraint enforcement, transport semantics. Mocking that out invalidates the test.

rule.statement: When db_engine_required == true AND commit_time_behavior_under_test == false, the emitted config MUST use rollback isolation.
rule.rationale: Rollback is the fastest correct choice when constraint-violation semantics aren't the explicit subject of the test.

rule.statement: When commit_time_behavior_under_test == true (e.g., unique-constraint flow), the emitted config MUST use truncate + transaction=True markers.
rule.rationale: Rollback hides commit-time errors; truncate exposes them.

rule.statement: When external_http_calls is 'few' or 'simple', the emitted config MUST use respx (no extra container).
rule.rationale: Transport interception is zero-overhead and reads naturally in test code.

rule.statement: When external_http_calls == 'many_or_complex', the emitted config MUST use a session-scoped WireMock container.
rule.rationale: A real HTTP server catches connection-pool, retry, and header-parsing bugs that mock transports miss.
